title: Carey Bloodworth
text: Home * People * Carey Bloodworth Carey Bloodworth , an American senior programmer, veteran Pi programmer, and also computer chess programmer [1] , computer chess historian and collector [2] . He worked with Tony Marsland to re-create Awit aka Wita, that it could execute on a Unix box [3] . Carey wrote some very fast pi calculation programs, the first one to compute one billion (2^30) decimal digits of pi on a x86 desktop computer in 1999 in less than 9 days [4] . His pi calculators also provide tutorials on high-precision and high-performance integer arithmetic [5] . Carey's abandoned computer chess sites Classic Computer Chess - ... The programs of yesteryear are saved by the Internet Archive [6] , and are often referred in the Chess Programming Wiki .
